figures.caption: Table 2: Variable selection and prediction performance for the ADNI and BlogFeedback datasets. “#Variables" indicates the number of selected variables. All metrics are reported by the means with standard deviations in parentheses.

Table 1: Simulation results for prediction, model complexity and variable selection for Setting 1. Predictive error (PE) and mean square error (MSE) are the model performance metrics. Dimension (Dims.) and proportion of zero elements in model parameters θ̂ (Prop.0) are the metrics for model complexity. The true positive rate (TPR) and false positive rate (FPR) are the metrics for variable selection. All metrics are reported by the means with standard deviations in parentheses.
Figure 4: First column: The image T-shirt and sneaker. Second column: Important pixels for predicting T-shirt. Last column: Important pixels for predicting sneaker.
Figure 3: First column: The digit 5 and 6. Second column: Important pixels for predicting digit 5. Last column: Important pixels for predicting digit 6.
Figure 1: Test power for informative variables and empirical size for noisy variables in Settings 1–4 under correlation levels ρ = 0.2.

Table 4: The variable selection and prediction performance of MNIST and Fashion MNIST dataset. “#Variables" means the number of selected variables. All metrics are reported by the means with standard deviations in parentheses.
Table 3: The selected genes and their p-values for ADNI analysis.

Figure 2: The distribution under H0 and the power of different representations with their singular value in Setting 3 (ρ = 0.2): (a) The distribution under H0; (b) The power of representations; (c) The singular value of representations.
